plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-297 RP08.P09.M04.S01-RP08.P09.M05.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-297 implements descriptor-only coverage of the full RP08.P09.M04 secondary workflow review cycle and the full M05 permission/audit binding review cycle (20 rows each) across two sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-298 handoff, without opening permission bypass, UI leaks, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, state writes, object storage, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-297 is a descriptor-only RP08.P09 review-slice pack (M04 Secondary Workflow Slice + M05 Permission And Audit Binding, 40 units) that passes independent read-only review. The pack makes no production_ready overclaim: manifest and command-evidence both record production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review, with the production_ready_flag being a descriptor-verification label and Hermes production_ready_candidate reflecting only coverage.valid && slice.valid; final readiness is deferred to human approval and downstream runtime-opening pack CP00-298. Deliverable distribution (claude_review:6, security_audit:4, test:2, ui:2, implementation:26 = 40) and micro-title distribution (20/20) match the baseline. All review rows are descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / raw_payload_included:false; the command_rerun guard (executes_command_runtime:false) is correctly inherited via the unbroken CP281->...->CP296->CP297 row-extras spread chain, so validator checks pass legitimately. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive (synthetic_only, no_real_data, no DB rows, no object storage, no audit-event writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command execution). Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required:true, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling, not embedded product runtime. CP178+ hardening is honored via forbidden_review_evidence (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) and a passing auth preflight with valid JSON. Contract projections (current_pack, cp297_requirements, cp297_no_write_attestation, review_slice_descriptor, historical_pack_bindings tail=CP00-297) are statically consistent with the code-generated structures deep-equaled by the validator; index.js wildcard re-exports propagate the new exports; tests cover coverage/descriptor/Hermes/Claude/handoff; README documents the pack. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are intentionally generated after this review per the documented gated sequence and are not a defect. No P0-P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-297 email dms core review slice descriptor pack
